title: Close workspace-sync smart_copy enumeration gaps (processed-reviews.json + 11 sibling gaps)
do_not_implement: Operator authorizes implementation in a separate step. This PROP edits no prompt files itself.
summary: Operator-spotted bug confirmed independently. monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md's smart_copy iteration omits monitor/decisions/processed-reviews.json — when decider's push 403s and universal-pusher copies the file to FUSE, workspace-sync never copies it back to git, so main's copy stays stale. digest-reviews.js builds processedSet from the stale copy on every decider run, so already-processed curmudgeon reviews keep appearing in pending-digest. The decider's staleness gate quietly skips them, but the cycle wastes Opus tokens and clutters daily-report commentary. Audit of monitor/decisions/, monitor/curmudgeon/, monitor/analyst/, monitor/integrity/, monitor/changes/, monitor/social/ surfaces 11 additional gap files with the same pattern (FUSE-newer-than-git for files agents currently write but workspace-sync's enumeration skips). Recommend an enhanced targeted fix: 13 smart_copy lines + 2 sync_glob lines. Defer the glob-based structural rewrite — the existing relic surface in workspace-only directories makes a 'iterate everything' approach actively dangerous.

independent_verification.operator_chain_audit: Operator's forensic chain re-derived from scratch. All steps pass.

step_1_digest_reviews_js_line.claim: build-scripts/digest-reviews.js line 33 reads PROCESSED_PATH from monitor/decisions/processed-reviews.json
step_1_digest_reviews_js_line.evidence: build-scripts/digest-reviews.js line 33: const PROCESSED_PATH = path.join(workspace, 'monitor/decisions/processed-reviews.json'); — confirmed verbatim. Lines 47–49 then load processedData and build the dedup Set.

step_2_workspace_sync_md_omission.claim: monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md has zero smart_copy reference to processed-reviews.json
step_2_workspace_sync_md_omission.evidence: grep -c 'processed-review' monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md → 0. The smart_copy iteration enumerates monitor/decisions/open-issues.json, monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json, monitor/decisions/morning-briefing.txt, monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json, but skips processed-reviews.json. NEVER_PUSH does NOT include it either, so the gap is purely an iteration omission, not an intentional deny.

step_3_fuse_vs_main_diff.claim: FUSE has 451 entries last_updated 2026-05-05T13:24Z; origin/main has 447 entries last_updated 2026-05-03T05:21Z (~58h stale)
step_3_fuse_vs_main_diff.evidence: FUSE: monitor/decisions/processed-reviews.json — 451 entries, last_updated 2026-05-05T13:24:04Z, last 5 entries WIN-031.c8.json, WIN-021.c3.json, WIN-014.c5.json, WIN-016.c6.json, WIN-017.c4.json. Main (via raw.githubusercontent.com): 447 entries, last_updated 2026-05-03T05:21:54Z, last 5 EXP-285-proposal.json, WIN-030.c4.json, EXP-283-proposal.c2.json, HNOTE-OPERATOR-FROZEN-PRED-REVIEW-RUBRIC-001-consolidation.json, WIN-031.c8.json. Delta: 4 entries authored on FUSE never reached main; staleness ~55h relative to fresh-clone git log time of 2026-05-03T05:24Z. The four missing entries (WIN-021.c3.json, WIN-014.c5.json, WIN-016.c6.json, WIN-017.c4.json) are exactly the reviews that decider has been re-processing as 'verified stale' on each run.

step_4_symptom_traced.claim: Decider 13:22 daily-report's 'all curmudgeon-flagged holes verified stale' is the symptom of this gap
step_4_symptom_traced.evidence: monitor/decisions/daily-report-2026-05-05T13-22.json patches_this_run.note matches operator's quote. The mechanism: digest-reviews.js loads stale processedSet from main → reviews already processed locally appear in pending-digest as un-processed → decider's staleness gate kicks in → decider closes the issue as stale and notes it in the daily report. This loop has been running for ~58h, repeating across each decider invocation.

independent_verification.operator_chain_corrections: None. Every step of the operator's evidence chain held up under independent verification.

gap_audit.scope: Per-file classification across monitor/decisions/, monitor/curmudgeon/, monitor/analyst/, monitor/integrity/, monitor/changes/, monitor/social/. Classifications: SMART_COPY (explicit one-line copy), SYNC_GLOB (covered by glob iteration), NEVER_PUSH (deny-listed), GAP (agent-written, FUSE-newer-than-git, NOT enumerated), RELIC (FUSE has it but agent prompts no longer reference; not currently written), GIT_ONLY (written from clone-side, no FUSE round-trip needed).

confirmed_gaps_agent_written.fuse_vs_git: FUSE +55h ahead of last main commit
confirmed_gaps_agent_written.blast_radius: Each decider run re-processes already-processed curmudgeon reviews; ~4 reviews × ~6 runs/day × ~3 days = ~70 redundant Opus staleness checks; clutters daily-report commentary

confirmed_gaps_agent_written.blast_radius: Dual-write rule is a workaround for this gap. Adding to smart_copy lets us delete the dual-write requirement.

confirmed_gaps_agent_written.consumer: decider-intake reads to spot critical/major holes
confirmed_gaps_agent_written.blast_radius: Decider's intake check sees stale alerts; misses recent critical findings until manually checked

confirmed_gaps_agent_written.blast_radius: Decider's intake reads stale summary; ~4.5 days behind. Less critical because decider also reads pending-digest, but contributes to context staleness.

confirmed_gaps_agent_written.blast_radius: Decider never sees integrity-flagged urgent alerts when reading from clone

confirmed_gaps_agent_written.blast_radius: Decider's intake context is missing daily integrity findings

confirmed_gaps_agent_written.writer: decider (clone-side write per decider.md line 242; universal-pusher rescues on 403)
confirmed_gaps_agent_written.blast_radius: Tinker's PROP-009 audit sees stale shadow log; under-reports recent bypasses. Note: ends in .jsonl not .json so the existing sync_glob on monitor/integrity/*.json doesn't catch it.

confirmed_gaps_agent_written.consumer: decider next-run dedup; tinker-cost-engineering ledger audit

Decider's intake context is missing latest analyst summary

confirmed_gaps_agent_written.writer: social agent (FUSE-side write per social.md line 142)

confirmed_gaps_agent_written.fuse_vs_git: git ahead -41h (operator's last edit reached git via clone-side write but FUSE has older copy)

confirmed_gaps_agent_written.consumer: tinker.md Mode 1 dispatcher checks every run for files >24h
confirmed_gaps_agent_written.blast_radius: Stranded-patches workflow assumes the stranded file reaches git so operator can apply on their own clone. Without sync_glob, the file is FUSE-only, and tinker's Mode 1 staleness check fires only on FUSE — operator can't see it from a clone.

relic_files_seen_but_not_proposed_for_sy.rationale: These exist in FUSE but agent prompts no longer reference them as active write paths. Adding sync coverage would re-introduce dead state to git. Operator may want to clean these up separately, but that's out-of-scope for this PROP.
files: monitor/decisions/suggested-patches.json (non-timestamped variant; superseded by suggested-patches-*.json glob)
relic_files_seen_but_not_proposed_for_sy.implication_for_structural_alternative: A 'iterate monitor/X/*.json' structural rewrite would force-sync ~15 relic files in monitor/analyst/ alone, plus several in monitor/decisions/ and monitor/curmudgeon/. That's the case AGAINST a flat glob-based structural rewrite.

gap_audit.sync_glob_recursion_note: sync_glob is intentionally NON-recursive (for f in ${WORKSPACE}/${rel}/${pat}; do — no **/ pattern). monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-runs/run-*.json is therefore not covered by the existing sync_glob monitor/integrity '*.json' line, but is fine because workspace-sync writes those files inside its own clone (Step 4b) and commits them git-side directly.

options_considered.name: Option A — flat glob-based iteration
options_considered.sketch: Replace explicit smart_copy enumeration with sync_glob monitor/decisions '*.json', sync_glob monitor/curmudgeon '*.json', etc. NEVER_PUSH remains the only filter.
pros: Self-extending: new agent write paths get caught automatically.
Eliminates the recurring 'someone added a write path and forgot to add it to workspace-sync' bug class entirely.
cons: Force-syncs ~15 relic files in monitor/analyst/ alone (wgs84-*, gravity-*, design-review-*, etc.) that are FUSE-only one-off forensic artifacts.
Force-syncs monitor/decisions/action-log.json and similar relics that have been quiet since 2026-04-06.
Pulls in agent scratch files (.tmp-*.js, __test_fuse.tmp) unless extension-filtered.
Hard-to-revert if a relic resurfaces after sync — the file is now in git history and might confuse readers.
options_considered.verdict: Rejected for this PROP. The relic surface is too large to absorb without a separate cleanup pass.

options_considered.name: Option B — sync manifest file
options_considered.sketch: Create monitor/sync-manifest.json with one entry per file/glob: {path, direction, owner_agent, notes}. workspace-sync iterates the manifest. Each agent owns its declarations; tinker/integrity agents lint that newly-introduced write paths in agent prompts are present in the manifest.
pros: Single source of truth for what crosses the workspace↔git boundary.
Lint-able: tinker/integrity could flag any new fs.writeFileSync path in prompts that isn't manifested.
Replaces the three-way duplication noted in CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules section: build.js OWNERSHIP, workspace-sync.md NEVER_PUSH, CLAUDE.md File Ownership table.
cons: Requires schema design and migration of all existing entries.
Adds a new file every agent has to know about.
Still needs the explicit work of populating it — same per-entry effort as the targeted fix, just in a different format.
options_considered.verdict: Worth doing as a follow-up PROP, NOT as the immediate fix. The targeted fix unblocks the stale-loop today; the manifest is a structural cleanup that needs its own design pass.

options_considered.name: Option C — integrity-side gap detector
options_considered.sketch: Add an integrity-agent check that walks monitor/X/ on FUSE, finds files with FUSE-mtime newer than main, and flags any not classified by workspace-sync. Doesn't fix the gap, but surfaces it within ~24h instead of waiting for someone to spot a stale-loop.
pros: Cheap — one extra check per integrity run.
cons: Detection only, no remediation. Operator still has to add lines manually.
Adds noise if relic-files end up flagged.
options_considered.verdict: Worth building alongside the targeted fix or as a follow-up. Not a substitute for fixing the gap today.

structural_alternative.recommendation: Targeted fix (Option D in the proposed_fix section). Defer Option B (sync manifest) to a separate PROP. Optionally add Option C (integrity-side gap detector) as a paired PR — see follow_up_recommendations below.

proposed_fix.approach: Enhanced targeted fix — add 13 explicit smart_copy lines and 2 sync_glob lines to monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md Step 2.
proposed_fix.rationale_vs_structural: Audit surfaced 12+ confirmed gaps (operator's threshold for 'structural becomes more attractive' was 3+). However, the workspace-only directories (monitor/analyst/, monitor/decisions/, monitor/curmudgeon/) carry ~15 relic files each that a flat glob-based rewrite would force-sync. The targeted fix avoids that risk while resolving every confirmed-active-writer gap. Operator's lean toward targeted is preserved; the audit just extends the line-count from 1 to 13.

diff_sketch.section: Step 2: Sync workspace files to clone
secondary_edits: Update CLAUDE.md 'Human Notes Rule' to remove the dual-write requirement now that workspace-sync round-trips all three human-notes.json files. The rule becomes: 'Human notes can be written to FUSE only; workspace-sync round-trips within ~1h.' Keep the dual-write option as belt-and-suspenders for urgent human notes that need to be visible immediately to a clone-reading agent.

proposed_fix.no_NEVER_PUSH_changes: Per non-goals: no additions to or removals from NEVER_PUSH. The audit found no file currently in NEVER_PUSH that should be eligible to push.
proposed_fix.no_digest_reviews_js_changes: Per non-goals: digest-reviews.js's staleness mechanism is correct. The bug is upstream in workspace-sync's enumeration.
proposed_fix.no_universal_pusher_changes: Per non-goals: decider's universal-pusher copies the file to FUSE correctly. The gap is on the FUSE→git return leg, which is workspace-sync's job.

measurement_plan.primary_metric: Time from agent-side FUSE write to git origin/main reflection
measurement_plan.pre_fix_baseline: monitor/decisions/processed-reviews.json: ~58h staleness on main (last_updated 2026-05-03T05:21Z vs FUSE 2026-05-05T13:24Z) at directive-issue time
measurement_plan.post_fix_target: Within 1 sync cycle (~4h, since workspace-sync runs every 4h) of fix deployment, processed-reviews.json on main should reflect the latest FUSE writes (last_updated should be ≤ 4h old).
verification_steps: After fix lands: wait for next workspace-sync run.
Check decider's next daily-report.patches_this_run.note — the 'verified stale' loop language should disappear (because pending-digest no longer has already-processed reviews).
Repeat for the other 12 gap files: each should have main-side mtime within 1-2 sync cycles of FUSE mtime.
disprove_conditions: If main's processed-reviews.json is still stale (last_updated older than 8h) after fix lands and workspace-sync has run twice, the fix is wrong. Investigate: maybe smart_copy mtime guard is rejecting the file (FUSE mtime not newer than git commit time), maybe NEVER_PUSH dynamic rule is matching unexpectedly, maybe sync_glob path is wrong.

If decider's daily-report keeps showing 'verified stale' after main is current, the bug is downstream of processed-reviews.json (maybe digest-reviews.js misparses the new entries, maybe pending-digest is cached somewhere else).
If a flood of relic files starts appearing in workspace-sync commits after the fix, the structural-alternative concern was warranted and a sync-manifest is needed urgently.
measurement_plan.monitoring_window: 1 week post-deployment. After 7 days of clean syncs, declare resolved.
measurement_plan.follow_up_after_resolution: Tinker should run a periodic FUSE-vs-main staleness audit (Mode 3 cost engineering) to catch any future enumeration drift before it becomes a stale-loop.

sequencing.single_session_implementation: Yes — one operator session with edit + commit + push to monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md, plus an optional companion edit to CLAUDE.md to update the Human Notes Rule.
rollout_steps: 1. Operator reads this PROP and the proposed diff.
2. Operator edits monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md to add the 13 smart_copy lines + 2 sync_glob lines.
3. (Optional) Operator edits CLAUDE.md to update the Human Notes Rule to reflect the new auto-sync behavior.
4. Operator commits and pushes from a clone (workspace-sync.md is in NEVER_PUSH dynamic rule, so it must be edited via clone).
5. Next workspace-sync invocation (within 4h) picks up the new prompt — but workspace-sync runs from its own clone with a fresh git pull, so the new prompt takes effect immediately.
6. Watch first run: confirm no flood of relic-file commits, confirm processed-reviews.json updates main.
sequencing.blast_radius: Touches one prompt file. Effect is additive (more files copied; nothing removed). Worst-case rollback: revert the workspace-sync.md edit; system returns to prior behavior.
sequencing.rollback_plan: git revert the prompt-edit commit; workspace-sync's next run uses the prior enumeration.
sequencing.no_dependencies: No other PROPs need to land first. No script changes. No agent prompt changes outside workspace-sync.md and (optionally) CLAUDE.md.

follow_up_recommendations.PROP_017_followup_A_sync_manifest: Author a separate PROP for Option B (sync manifest file) as a longer-term structural cleanup. Eliminates the three-way duplication of file ownership classifications across build.js, workspace-sync.md, and CLAUDE.md. Requires schema design + migration; not blocking the immediate fix.
follow_up_recommendations.PROP_017_followup_B_integrity_gap_detect: Author a separate PROP for Option C (integrity-side gap detector). Adds a per-run check that flags any FUSE-newer-than-main file not classified by workspace-sync. Catches future enumeration drift in <24h instead of waiting for a human to spot a stale-loop.
follow_up_recommendations.claude_md_human_notes_rule_amendment: Once PROP-017 lands, update CLAUDE.md Human Notes Rule from 'dual-write to FUSE and git clone' to 'write to FUSE; workspace-sync round-trips within 4h. Dual-write remains an option for urgent notes that must reach clone-reading agents immediately.'
follow_up_recommendations.relic_cleanup_optional: monitor/analyst/ and monitor/decisions/ have ~15 relic files (one-off forensic outputs, dead writers since 2026-04-06). Consider a separate cleanup pass to either delete them or archive to monitor/_archive/. Not in scope for this PROP.

non_goals_compliance.no_NEVER_PUSH_changes: Confirmed. Audit found no file currently in NEVER_PUSH that should be eligible to push.
non_goals_compliance.no_digest_reviews_js_changes: Confirmed. The staleness mechanism is correct; bug is in workspace-sync.
non_goals_compliance.no_universal_pusher_changes: Confirmed. Universal-pusher correctly copies to FUSE; bug is on the FUSE→git return leg.
non_goals_compliance.no_schedule_changes: Confirmed. Cadence is fine; coverage is the problem.
non_goals_compliance.no_other_agent_write_path_changes: Confirmed. All proposed changes are in monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md (and optionally CLAUDE.md for the human-notes documentation update).

completion_criteria_self_check.audit_complete: Yes — see gap_audit section. Per-file classification table for monitor/decisions/, monitor/curmudgeon/, monitor/analyst/, monitor/integrity/, monitor/changes/, monitor/social/. Confirmed gaps listed; covered paths listed; relic files surfaced.
completion_criteria_self_check.fix_specified: Yes — see proposed_fix section. 13 smart_copy lines + 2 sync_glob lines, with placement guidance for each.
completion_criteria_self_check.measurement_pre_committed: Yes — see measurement_plan. Disprove conditions named.
completion_criteria_self_check.directive_marked_completed: Pending — tinker will mark DIRECTIVE-20260505-001 status:completed in this same run per DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE additive-edit exception.

monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md — the file that needs the fix

verification_pattern.purpose: Used by tinker's PROP Lifecycle Verification (Mode 1 / previous_followup) to decide if PROP-017 is FIXED or STILL_BROKEN.
verification_pattern.primary_test: After PROP-017 lands, check that monitor/decisions/processed-reviews.json on origin/main has last_updated within 8h of the latest FUSE-side last_updated. Specifically: curl raw.githubusercontent.com → parse last_updated → compare to FUSE last_updated → diff should be ≤ 8h.
verification_pattern.secondary_test: Also check that monitor/analyst/attention-inbox.json on main has updated since 2026-05-05T08:00Z (the directive-time FUSE timestamp) — confirms the audit caught the right siblings, not just the primary case.
verification_pattern.still_broken_reason_template: Stale on main: file=<path>, fuse_last_updated=<ts>, main_last_updated=<ts>, delta_hours=<n>. Either the smart_copy line was not added, the mtime guard is rejecting the file, or workspace-sync hasn't run since the fix landed.
